001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.MarshallUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.test.bct.BctAssertions.*;
022import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.test.bct.*;
025
026/**
027 * Utility class for testing bean functionality in a standardized way.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * This class provides a fluent API for setting up and executing common bean tests including:
031 * <ul>
032 *    <li>Getter/setter validation using {@link BctAssertions#assertBean(Object, String, String)}
033 *    <li>Copy constructor/method testing
034 *    <li>JSON serialization/deserialization testing
035 *    <li>Round-trip serialization testing
036 *    <li>toString() method validation
037 * </ul>
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Example Usage:</h5>
040 * <p class='bjava'>
041 *    BeanTester&lt;MyBean&gt; tester = testBean(myBean)
042 *       .json("{prop1:'value1',prop2:'value2'}")
043 *       .props("prop1,prop2")
044 *       .vals("'value1','value2'")
045 *       .string("{prop1:'value1',prop2:'value2'}");
046 *
047 *    tester.assertGettersAndSetters();
048 *    tester.assertToJson();
049 *    tester.assertFromJson();
050 *    tester.assertRoundTrip();
051 *    tester.assertToString();
052 * </p>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * This class is typically used in nested test classes to provide consistent testing patterns
056 * across different bean types in the Apache Juneau project.
057 *
058 * @param <T> The type of bean being tested
059 */
060@SuppressWarnings({
061   "unchecked" // Class<T> cast for generic bean type in test utility
062})
063public class BeanTester<T> {
064
065   private T bean;
066   private Class<T> c;
067
068   /**
069    * Sets the bean instance to be tested.
070    *
071    * @param value The bean instance to test.
072    * @return This object for method chaining.
073    */
074   public BeanTester<T> bean(T value) { bean = value; c = (Class<T>) bean.getClass(); return this; }
075
076   /**
077    * Returns the bean instance being tested.
078    *
079    * @return The bean instance being tested.
080    */
081   public T bean() { return bean; }
082
083   private String json;
084
085   /**
086    * Sets the expected JSON representation of the bean.
087    *
088    * <p>
089    * This JSON string is used by {@link #assertToJson()} to verify that the bean
090    * serializes to the expected JSON format, and by {@link #assertFromJson()} to
091    * verify that the JSON deserializes back to an equivalent bean.
092    *
093    * @param value The expected JSON representation.
094    * @return This object for method chaining.
095    */
096   public BeanTester<T> json(String value) { json = value; return this; }
097
098   /**
099    * Returns the expected JSON representation of the bean.
100    *
101    * @return The expected JSON representation.
102    */
103   public String json() { return json; }
104
105   private String props;
106
107   /**
108    * Sets the comma-delimited list of property names for bean validation.
109    *
110    * <p>
111    * This string is used by {@link BctAssertions#assertBean(Object, String, String)} to
112    * validate that the bean has the expected properties with the expected values.
113    * Property names should be listed in the same order as their corresponding values
114    * in the {@link #vals(String)} parameter.
115    *
116    * @param value Comma-delimited list of property names (e.g., "prop1,prop2,prop3").
117    * @return This object for method chaining.
118    */
119   public BeanTester<T> props(String value) { props = value; return this; }
120
121   /**
122    * Returns the comma-delimited list of property names.
123    *
124    * @return The comma-delimited list of property names.
125    */
126   public String props() { return props; }
127
128   private String vals;
129
130   /**
131    * Sets the comma-delimited list of expected property values for bean validation.
132    *
133    * <p>
134    * This string is used by {@link BctAssertions#assertBean(Object, String, String)} to
135    * validate that the bean properties have the expected values. Values should be
136    * listed in the same order as their corresponding property names in the
137    * {@link #props(String)} parameter. String values should be quoted (e.g., "'value'").
138    *
139    * @param value Comma-delimited list of expected values (e.g., "'value1','value2',123").
140    * @return This object for method chaining.
141    */
142   public BeanTester<T> vals(String value) { vals = value; return this; }
143
144   /**
145    * Returns the comma-delimited list of expected property values.
146    *
147    * @return The comma-delimited list of expected property values.
148    */
149   public String vals() { return vals; }
150
151   private String string;
152
153   /**
154    * Sets the expected string representation of the bean.
155    *
156    * <p>
157    * This string is used by {@link #assertToString()} to verify that the bean's
158    * {@code toString()} method returns the expected value.
159    *
160    * @param value The expected string representation.
161    * @return This object for method chaining.
162    */
163   public BeanTester<T> string(String value) { string = value; return this; }
164
165   /**
166    * Returns the expected string representation of the bean.
167    *
168    * @return The expected string representation.
169    */
170   public String string() { return string; }
171
172   /**
173    * Validates that the bean's getters and setters work correctly.
174    *
175    * <p>
176    * This method uses {@link BctAssertions#assertBean(Object, String, String)} to verify
177    * that the bean's properties match the expected values specified by {@link #props(String)}
178    * and {@link #vals(String)}.
179    *
180    * @throws AssertionError if any property values don't match expectations.
181    */
182   public void assertGettersAndSetters() {
183      assertBean(bean, props, vals);
184   }
185
186   /**
187    * Validates that the bean's copy constructor or copy() method works correctly.
188    *
189    * <p>
190    * This method uses reflection to invoke the bean's {@code copy()} method, then verifies:
191    * <ul>
192    *    <li>The returned object is not the same instance as the original
193    *    <li>The returned object has the same property values as the original
194    * </ul>
195    *
196    * @throws AssertionError if the copy is the same instance or has different property values.
197    * @throws RuntimeException if the bean doesn't have a {@code copy()} method or it fails to invoke.
198    */
199   public void assertCopy() {
200      var t = safe(()->bean.getClass().getMethod("copy").invoke(bean));
201      assertNotSame(bean, t);
202      assertBean(t, props, vals);
203   }
204
205   /**
206    * Validates that the bean serializes to the expected JSON format.
207    *
208    * <p>
209    * This method verifies that the bean serializes (via JSON5) to the JSON string
210    * specified by {@link #json(String)}.
211    *
212    * @throws AssertionError if the serialized JSON doesn't match the expected format.
213    */
214   public void assertToJson() {
215      assertEquals(json, json5(bean));
216   }
217
218   /**
219    * Validates that the expected JSON deserializes to a bean with correct property values.
220    *
221    * <p>
222    * This method deserializes the JSON string specified by {@link #json(String)} and
223    * verifies that the resulting bean has the expected property values specified by
224    * {@link #props(String)} and {@link #vals(String)}.
225    *
226    * @throws AssertionError if the deserialized bean doesn't have the expected property values.
227    * @throws RuntimeException if JSON deserialization fails.
228    */
229   public void assertFromJson() {
230      assertBean(json(json, c), props, vals);
231   }
232
233   /**
234    * Validates that the bean can be serialized to JSON and deserialized back correctly.
235    *
236    * <p>
237    * This method performs a complete round-trip test by:
238    * <ol>
239    *    <li>Serializing the bean to JSON
240    *    <li>Deserializing the JSON back to a bean
241    *    <li>Verifying the resulting bean has the expected property values
242    * </ol>
243    *
244    * @throws AssertionError if the round-trip bean doesn't have the expected property values.
245    * @throws RuntimeException if serialization or deserialization fails.
246    */
247   public void assertRoundTrip() {
248      assertBean(jsonRoundTrip(bean, c), props, vals);
249   }
250
251   /**
252    * Validates that the bean's toString() method returns the expected string.
253    *
254    * <p>
255    * This method compares the bean's {@code toString()} output with the string
256    * specified by {@link #string(String)}.
257    *
258    * @throws AssertionError if the toString() output doesn't match the expected string.
259    */
260   public void assertToString() {
261      assertEquals(string, bean.toString());
262   }
263
264   /**
265    * Serializes an object to JSON using the default sorted JSON serializer.
266    *
267    * @param o The object to serialize.
268    * @return The JSON representation of the object.
269    */
270   private static String json(Object o) {
271      return json5(o);
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Deserializes a JSON string to an object of the specified type.
276    *
277    * @param <T> The type to deserialize to.
278    * @param o The JSON string to deserialize.
279    * @param c The class to deserialize to.
280    * @return The deserialized object.
281    */
282   private static <T> T json(String o, Class<T> c) {
283      return safe(()->json5(o, c));
284   }
285
286   /**
287    * Performs a round-trip serialization/deserialization test.
288    *
289    * @param <T> The type of object to round-trip.
290    * @param o The object to serialize and deserialize.
291    * @param c The class to deserialize to.
292    * @return The object after round-trip serialization/deserialization.
293    */
294   private static <T> T jsonRoundTrip(T o, Class<T> c) {
295      return json(json(o), c);
296   }
297}
